journal_entry.description: Conversion of bank loan to equity, issuing 400 common shares
journal_entry.rationale: The company converted $200 of loan payable to equity by issuing 400 common shares. This is a debt-to-equity conversion transaction. Debit Loan Payable (liability decrease, slot d=1) to remove the liability. Credit Common Shares (equity increase, slot b=1) to record the issuance. No cash changes hands in a conversion. This is a non-taxable capital transaction. The $200 is allocated entirely to common shares at $0.50 per share (400 shares). Total debits ($200) = total credits ($200).

output.reason: Loan payable (liability) is reduced by converting debt to equity. Crediting a liability account decreases it = liability decrease. Since the credit tuple only tracks increases (liability increase in slot a), a liability decrease does NOT appear in the credit tuple. Instead, the liability is debited. On the credit side: common shares (equity) increases = equity increase (slot b, 1 line).

Since the credit tuple only tracks increases (liability increase in slot a), a liability decrease does NOT appear in the credit tuple. Instead, the liability is debited. On the credit side: common shares (equity) increases = equity increase (slot b, 1 line).

output.reason: Converting a bank loan to equity means the loan liability is eliminated. Debit: Liability decrease (loan payable reduced by $200). The issuance of common shares is the credit side (equity increase), which we do not classify here.
